titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 714

div.p: Let present and future know that I, William de Vaux, have given, and granted, and by this my present charter have made firm to God and Saint Cuthbert of Durham and the holy monks serving God and Saint Cuthbert there, one toft in Gullane, that is the toft which lies nearest the croft of the hospital of St John of Jerusalem towards the south, as long as the aforesaid croft of Saint John is broad; and one perch of land which Udard the fisherman held in the aforementioned toun, that is the perch of land which lies nearest the croft of William Smallware towards the north, and the northern edge of Swindgthates, as it has gone out from Gullane towards the large stone which lies outside Windgthates, towards the north, and so upwards as the land of Saint Cuthbert was perambulated and measured. I also will that Osmund of Gullane and his heirs should hold the aforesaid toft, and the aforementioned perch of land, and the aforementioned edge, as freely, quitly, and honourably as any alms is most freely and quitly held or possessed in the whole kindgom of Scotland, by rendering annually to the aforesaid monks four pence at the feast of Saint Michael in return for every service. With these witnesses, Robert Hotoft, Gregory of Fenton, Walter of Lindsay, Richard Sans Avoir, Richard son of Seleburh, Godard the chaplain, Gummer the chaplain, Simon the clerk, Gilbert the butler of Gullane, Robert son of Adam of Dirleton.
